title: The Iran War Is Taking the Color Out of Japan’s Best-Known Snack Bags
summary_full: The food giant Calbee said shortages of naphtha, a crude-oil derivative used in inks, were forcing it to switch to black-and-white packaging for its salty products.
summary_public: The food giant Calbee said shortages of naphtha, a crude-oil derivative used in inks, were forcing it to switch to black-and-white packaging for its salty products.

IR.label: negative
IR.evidence: The Iran War Is Taking the Color Out
